title: Policy and graph descriptors
commands_run: shellcheck --severity=warning bin/archon bin/archon-sync bin/archon-validate lib/common.sh lib/descriptor.sh lib/registry.sh
outcome: Policy and graph descriptors. Authored no-agent-git-push.policy.toml (agents never push/publish: [block] commands git push variants + tools gh release/merge; [allow] commands status/diff/log/fetch/add/commit; [rationale]) and policy.schema.json (draft-07, permissive — accepts both agent-rails style [agent]/[gates]/[secrets] and command style [block]/[allow], required: id+version, id pattern enforced). Graph: edges.schema.json (draft-07, nodes id+kind enum, edges from+rel enum+to) + graphs/README.md (directory contract). lib/registry.sh gains archon_emit_graph (derives graph.json from unit capabilities + policy applies_to: unit-provides-capability, unit-requires-capability, unit-governed-by-policy, agent-blocked-by-policy; 14 nodes, 11 edges) and archon_emit_graph_dot (Graphviz DOT rendering). bin/archon-sync emits graph.json + graph.dot after units/policies are written. bin/archon-validate extended with _validate_policies (required keys + id pattern vs policy.schema.json) and _validate_graph (required keys, node kind enum, edge rel enum, dangling endpoint check vs edges.schema.json). Docs updated: metadata-plane.md, archon README, archon AGENTS. Also wrote Build/bin/E04_PROGRESS_NOTES.md documenting S1-S3 detail + S4 + remaining plan.
validation: GATE moon run archon:validate PASS — 3 descriptors + 2 policies (dust.agent, no-agent-git-push) + graph (14 nodes, 11 edges, all endpoints resolve, no dangling). moon run archon:sync PASS (graph.json + graph.dot generated). Regression: moon run dust:validate-substrate exit 0. shellcheck --severity=warning CLEAN on all 6 bash files.
decisions: graph is fully derived (not hand-authored) from descriptor capabilities + policy applies_to — the emitter reads units.json + policies.json that sync already generated
governance edges: unit -governed-by-> policy when policy.applies_to == unit.id (dust.agent applies_to=dust links the dust unit)
actor edges: agent -blocked-by-> policy when policy.applies_to == agent (no-agent-git-push creates an actor node)
DOT is a rendering of the JSON graph (single source); blank lines between edges are cosmetic and Graphviz ignores them

policy.schema.json is permissive (additionalProperties:true, accepts both policy styles) so the existing dust.agent.policy.toml and the new no-agent-git-push.policy.toml both validate under one schema
validator checks graph integrity (dangling endpoints) not just schema conformance — every edge from/to must reference an existing node
next: E04 COMPLETE. Next: E05.S1 — agent profiles (populates .agents/profiles/ + profiles.json)
token_note: graph query (jq over graph.json) answers 'what depends on this capability' or 'what policy governs this unit' without re-reading descriptors; the graph is derived and compact, cheaper than its source
